title: Manager, Community Outreach
description:
What’s on Offer

  • Mission: Provide compassionate, excellent and holistic healthcare. Tagline:- Serve all with Love.

  • Company: Guided by Catholic values, as a missionary not-for-profit hospital, we reach out and serve all our patients and visitors with love, continue to make private healthcare accessible to our community by providing reasonable price points and bill transparency. We continue to be progressive to better serve current and future generations.

  • Values: Compassion, Humility, Integrity, Respect and Peace (CHIRP).

  • Work Satisfaction: An impactful role in shaping a high performing & engaging culture.

Who We Need

Central to Mount Alvernia Hospital’s mission is a commitment to giving back to the community and serving the vulnerable. This legacy is being carried forward by the Community Outreach Team. Join our burgeoning team to drive the growth in our hospital-based programme providing highly subsidised tertiary care. In this role, you will: 

  • Spearhead the expansion of our existing hospital-based programme to serve low-income transnational families in Singapore.

  • Build channels of referrals from social service agencies in identified new target segments of persons with disabilities and mental health for vulnerable youths.

  • Formulate programme policies, organise operating procedures and develop strategies to meet long-term goals.

  • Build and nurture strong relationship with key stakeholders including social workers, volunteer doctors and hospital management on outreach programmes.

  • Develop a cloud-based case management and data repository for the tracking of cases and analysis of statistics for KPI reporting.

  • Collaborate closely with clinic- based and offsite Outreach colleagues to bring about a holistic outreach healthcare offering for the community.

How to Succeed

  • Bachelor’s degree in any discipline.

  • At least 5 years of experience in social services especially in programme management.

  • Healthcare experience advantageous.

  • Strong project management skills with demonstrated ability to drive initiatives from conceptualisation to
    implementation.

  • Detail oriented, possess strong reasoning with the ability to manage multiple projects and meet tight
    deadlines.

  • Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to build rapport with diverse stakeholders.

  • Self-driven, thrives on teamwork with a heart to serve all with love.

postedCompany.description: Mount Alvernia Hospital is the first private Catholic-based not-for-profit acute care tertiary hospital. Our doctors from every medical specialty work together to care for patients, joined by common system and shared philosophy of "serving all with love." Established in 1961, the 367-bedded hospital is supported by more than 1,500 specialists covering about 28 specialties, with about 150 specialists locating their clinics within the hospital. Through our outreach medical clinic, we serve over 70 communities in Singapore.
postedCompany.name: MOUNT ALVERNIA HOSPITAL
